area_anchor: DO workflow compact process topology schema
current_truth: DO packets currently serialize walkthrough sections as generic markdown-table row objects with repeated header keys. The data is deterministic, but it is not yet TS-owned in a compact shape, so the process subpayload is not materially smaller than the markdown section it represents.
next_scope_now: Replace stage-specific walkthrough row arrays with a compact typed process_topology schema in skill-runner, update the contract to that schema, regenerate packets, and prove the byte reduction on a persisted workflow slug.
adjacent_later: Add packet byte-budget checks if compact topology still drifts upward
Derive more plan process topology fields from task graph metadata rather than markdown prose
evidence_refs: operator-stated: the biggest remaining token-efficiency win is lifting more of this into .ts work
why: The workflow now has the right walkthrough checkpoints, but the packet payload is still carrying repeated markdown column names instead of a compact TS-owned topology shape. That leaves a real token-efficiency gap even after the earlier packet work.

intended_outcome.statement: DO packets carry a compact typed process_topology payload for process-changing work, and regenerated packets for a proof slug show a smaller topology subpayload than the current raw row-object shape.
